title: carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei
intro: Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei, commonly known as Matsue daisy, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Japan, where it is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of this plant involve its ability to alleviate fever, relieve pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. The bioactive constituents of Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
benefits: Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei has many benefits, such as its ability to help in the treatment of various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. This plant is also rich in antioxidants, which can aid in protecting the body from oxidative stress and cell damage, thereby promoting overall well-being and a longer lifespan. Its antiseptic properties can help in wound healing, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a faster recovery, while its anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Overall, the benefits of Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei plant can significantly improve the quality of life by providing relief from various health issues and promoting a healthier and more resilient body.
actions: Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. These properties allow it to improve wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and fight off infections, ultimately improving daily lives by facilitating faster recovery from injuries and illnesses. Additionally, its antiseptic and antibacterial actions help prevent the growth of harmful microorganisms, reducing the risk of infections and promoting a healthier environment, especially in hospitals and healthcare settings. By addressing various health concerns, Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei contributes to improved overall well-being, enabling individuals to engage in physical activities, pursue their passions, and live more comfortably.
intro_study: According to "Biochemical pharmacology", Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei has anti-inflammatory properties, which may be due in part to its ability to inhibit inducible NO synthase (iNOS) gene expression.
constituents: Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei has many active constituents, such as sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These constituents, including carpesin, carpesone, and carpesalol, can help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce the risk of cancer, and protect against neurodegenerative diseases, ultimately improving the quality of life for individuals. The plant's flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, can also help to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, while its phenolic acids can aid in the prevention of certain types of cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
parts: Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and flowers. The leaves of Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei are known to contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of this plant contain sesquiterpene lactones and coumarins, which have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei contain sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties.
side_effects: Carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin irritation and respiratory issues. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can lead to more severe side effects, including liver damage and kidney problems. Some individuals may also experience headaches, dizziness, and fatigue after ingesting the plant. Additionally, carpesium divaricatum var. matsuei may interact with certain medications, exacerbating their side effects or reducing their efficacy.
